summary.brief: Leslie Coleman v. United States. Medical malpractice case involving surgery general.

evidence_index.text: Coleman was in the Air Force from 1999 to 2005. In 2005, she had a laparoscopic gastric band ('lap band') inserted by a private medical facility.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In April 2014, Coleman went to the Audie L. Murphy Memorial VA Hospital in San Antonio (VAH-SA), where she complained of daily vomiting and reflux.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In March 2016, a VAH-SA physician surgically removed the lap band.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In May 2016, a SAMMC physician attempted to perform the sleeve gastrectomy, but aborted the procedure mid-surgery after concluding that the risk of post-operative complications was too high.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In July 2016, Coleman went to a private medical facility where the sleeve gastrectomy was ultimately performed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: To support some of her medical malpractice claims, Coleman offered reports from two medical experts, Dr. Saini and Dr. Flancbaum.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court held that she could not meet her threshold burden for establishing the relevant standard of care, and consequently the court granted summary judgment to the government on Coleman's FTCA medical malpractice claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court determined that Coleman had not produced sufficient evidence that the alleged disclosures were harmful or willful.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court's summary judgment for the government on the three privacy-related claims that were reached on their merits is AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court was correct in its determination that Federal Rule of Evidence 601 requires that Coleman's proffered expert witness must satisfy the state law standards for expert witness competency.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports daily vomiting and reflux, seeking removal of lap band and revision to sleeve gastrectomy.

requestables.name: Upper Endoscopy
requestables.description: Endoscopic examination to assess lap band position

reveal.result_summary: No evidence of lap band slipping
reveal.clinical_significance: Decision to leave lap band in place
reveal.decision_impact: Led to continued symptoms and delayed removal

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with symptoms related to lap band

key_events.event: Patient reports daily vomiting and reflux

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Endoscopy performed to assess lap band

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with daily vomiting and reflux, suspected lap band complication
decision_points.prompt: Should the lap band be removed based on symptoms?

options.clinical_reasoning: Symptoms suggestive of lap band complication warrant removal

options.description: Leave lap band in place based on endoscopy findings
options.clinical_reasoning: Endoscopy showed no slipping, but symptoms persisted

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant left lap band in place
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Endoscopy findings did not show slipping

expected_action_court.description: Lap band should have been removed

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Persistent symptoms indicated a need for removal despite endoscopy results
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Lap band removal should have been prioritized
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Lap band eventually removed, sleeve gastrectomy performed at private facility

key_findings: Plaintiff could not establish the relevant standard of care due to lack of expert testimony

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to remove lap band despite symptoms
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Removal could have alleviated symptoms earlier

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with daily vomiting and reflux
Upper endoscopy performed, lap band not removed
Sleeve gastrectomy performed at private facility

tests_performed.result: No evidence of lap band slipping

allegations.allegation: Negligence in treating slipped lap band

court_findings.finding: Plaintiff could not establish the relevant standard of care
